_comment: Single source of truth for site content. index.html is hand-authored to match this file; update both together. All claims trace to the author's CV / curated achievement summaries and verified public sources (see WEBSITE_PLAN.md).

profile.subtagline: Full-Stack AI Infrastructure for Efficient and Scalable AI
profile.positioning: I build the computing systems that make large-scale AI practical and efficient — spanning accelerator-aware inference runtimes, GPU-cluster training, AI-datacenter networking, and cloud and secure systems.
profile.affiliation: AI Systems Research at Samsung SDS

pillars.title: Accelerator-aware inference & runtimes
pillars.body: Real-hardware processing-in-memory scheduling and matrix mapping, low-precision kernels, and LLM serving.

pillars.title: GPU-cluster & systems-for-ML training
pillars.body: Convergence-safe, efficient training across heterogeneous GPU fleets; GPU-as-a-Service.

pillars.title: AI-datacenter networking
pillars.body: End-host RDMA/RoCEv2 congestion control for GPU interconnect at fabric scale.

pillars.title: Cloud, OS & secure systems
pillars.body: Cloud platforms and storage, persistent memory, and confidential computing for secure AI execution.
pillars.systems: Samsung Cloud Platform · TIPS · Nested Enclaves

selected_research.title: PAISE
selected_research.problem: Decoder-only LLM inference is memory-bound: attention re-reads a growing KV cache and stalls the GPU on HBM bandwidth.
selected_research.contribution: A per-layer GPU-vs-PIM offloading scheduler with data-layout adjustment and an interleaved-batched GEMM kernel on real HBM-PIM — up to 48.3% faster than GPU-only.
selected_research.role: Senior (last) author; set the direction and the offloading-scheduling algorithm and cost model.

selected_research.title: Omni-MP
selected_research.problem: Real PIM accepts only fixed matrix tiles, so prior PIM-for-LLM work accelerated only feed-forward layers, not attention.
selected_research.contribution: Generalized batched PIM-GEMV mapping (interlacing/cutting), zero-tile skipping, and PIM-aware KV concatenation — 40.2% average end-to-end speedup on real hardware.
selected_research.role: Senior co-author and real-PIM program lead; set the generalization goal.

selected_research.title: JABAS
selected_research.problem: Training on heterogeneous GPU clusters wastes resources, and the usual remedy breaks the i.i.d. assumption and hurts convergence.
selected_research.contribution: IIDP preserves convergence via virtual stream workers while batching and GPU allocation are tuned jointly — −33.3% time, −54.2% cost, no accuracy loss.
selected_research.role: Industry co-author (collaboration with UNIST); supplied the production heterogeneous-GPU problem and real-cluster validation context — did not lead the algorithm design.

selected_research.title: CORN
selected_research.problem: RoCEv2 leans on switch-level PFC, which causes head-of-line blocking and PFC storms at the scale of AI GPU fabrics.
selected_research.contribution: End-host, RTT/BDP-driven congestion control with selective-ACK recovery makes RDMA run PFC-free and switch-transparent — zero PFC events in evaluation.
selected_research.role: Senior (last) author; set the cloud-RDMA direction and the RTT-based congestion-detection idea. Implemented as a hardware-NIC prototype in collaboration with AMD and demonstrated at SC25.

selected_research.title: DSDE
selected_research.problem: A fixed speculation length in speculative decoding wastes compute and creates batch stragglers under diverse serving load.
selected_research.contribution: A training-free, KLD-variance-driven per-sequence speculation length with an adaptive cap, implemented in vLLM — robust in low-acceptance-rate regimes.
selected_research.role: Senior co-author and research lead; drove the adaptive speculation-length cap and the vLLM integration.

open_source.name: Local-Inference-System (LIS)
open_source.desc: CPU-only local inference runtime focused on correctness, inspectability, reproducible artifacts, and transparent diagnostics.

open_source.name: llm-avx-lab
open_source.desc: Educational AVX2/FMA kernels for understanding LLM serving primitives from scalar C to SIMD.

experience.detail: Set a full-stack AI-infrastructure research agenda — GPU-as-a-Service / x.Cloud, PIM-accelerated inference, and AI-datacenter networking — and built a publishing systems-research program (HPCA, IEEE BigData, MCCSys@ICS).
GPU-accelerated NFV data plane (GPUDirect RDMA), cloud-native 5G vRAN, and persistent-memory systems.
Dissertation: Virtual Platforms — System Support to Enrich the Functionality of End Client Devices. Advisor: Prof. Karsten Schwan.
Mechanical Design and Production Engineering / Mechanical Engineering.
